title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Quantum mechanics is the branch of physics that describes the behaviour of matter and energy at the subatomic level, a domain (0) which defies the intuitions built up through everyday experience. The theory emerged in the early twentieth century and has never been surpassed in its predictive accuracy. (9) Although its mathematical formalism is well established, the philosophical implications of quantum mechanics remain a source of deep and unresolved debate. (10) Such is the counterintuitive nature of quantum behaviour that even physicists working daily with its equations often describe a sense of wonder at what it implies. Experimental physicists have confirmed its predictions with extraordinary precision, and (11) Provided that the relevant mathematical conditions are satisfied, quantum mechanical predictions prove reliable to many decimal places. Not (12) only does the theory govern the behaviour of atoms and electrons, but it also underlies the technology of transistors, lasers and magnetic resonance imaging. (13) Regardless of the philosophical framework one adopts to interpret its meaning, the empirical success of quantum mechanics is beyond dispute. Not (14) until the development of quantum computing began in earnest did engineers start to exploit quantum superposition and entanglement for practical information processing. (15) Given the profound strangeness of its implications, it is perhaps unsurprising that physicists themselves disagree about what the theory ultimately tells us about the nature of reality. These interpretational debates continue to this day. (16) Whether reality is fundamentally probabilistic or whether hidden variables remain to be discovered, quantum mechanics will continue to define the frontier of scientific understanding.

example.text: Quantum mechanics is the branch of physics that describes the behaviour of matter and energy at the subatomic level, a domain (0) which defies the intuitions built up through everyday experience.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Preposition used as a discourse marker meaning 'taking into account' or 'considering the fact that', introducing an explanatory concession.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
